content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Pat Eklund 
Novato City Councilmember 
922 Machin A venue 
Novato, CA 94945 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-079 
Dear Ms. Eklund: 
March 27, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Acl").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section I 090. 
QUESTION 
May you participate in decisions relating to the acquisition of a city-owned parcel by PG&E, 
including decisions relating to the eminent domain proceedings and permits, if any, needed by 
PG&E to establish a PG&E pipe testing facility despite owning property within 708 feel of the site? 
CONCLUSION 
We conclude, based on the facts presented, that you may participate in the decisions because 
they will not have a foreseeable and material financial effect on your real property. 
FACTS 
You are a member of the Novato City Council. The City Council will be considering a 
proposed PG&E project to be located on City-owned property on South Novato Blvd. The property 
is less than an acre (.89 acres). The property is unimproved and currently zoned parkland. However, 
parcels immediately adjacent to the site are zoned residential. 
1 The Political Reform Acl is contained in Govcrnmcnl Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All s1a1u1ory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicah:d. 


File No. A-17-079 
Page No. 2 
On March 28, 2017, the City Council will be meeting in closed session to consider PG&E's 
Eminent Domain proceeding to acquire a parcel of real property owned by the City. The City­
owned parcel is currently vacant open space and was acquired as park property through dedication 
from the subdivider of the Hillside Park Phase 1 development. 
You own property (a townhome in Hillside Park East or Hillside Park 2) that is 708 feet 
from the property PG&E is seeking to acquire. You currently rent the townhome to a tenant. The 
townhome is on the south side of South Novato Blvd (the same side as the subject parcel). In 
addition, your townhome is separated from the subject property (as the crow flies) by Redwood 
Boulevard and Hillside Park Phase 1 (several buildings with multiple town house units) as well as 
trees and other landscaping. The City Parcel cannot be seen from your townhome. 
After acquisition of the parcel, PG&E plans to construct on it a pipeline testing facility. You 
stated that the PG&E facility will primarily be located underground but the visual character of the 
property will change from an open grassy field to a site fully fenced, graveled and containing some 
visible pipe and utility cabinet facilities. PG&E will landscape the City Parcel outside of the fence 
for visual screening. 
1t is not anticipated that significant traffic impacts will result from the PG&E Facility 
project in that employees of PG&E will only visit the site occasionally for inspection purposes. 
These inspections will be performed by employees of PG&E who will arrive in one or two vehicles 
and who will stay at the City Parcel a minimum amount of time. Once every seven years, PG&E 
employees will bring in larger equipment and trucks to perform certain testing at the City Parcel. 
During construction, it is anticipated that traffic on South Novato Blvd. will be subject to 
traffic control activity which could cause temporary delays during the work day. This activity will 
cease after construction within the roadway and adjacent thereto is complete. Once the PG&E 
Facility is constructed, the public will no longer have access to the City Parcel. 
It is not likely that significant noise or odor impacts will occur from the operations of the 
PG&E FaciJity. Noise and dust may be present in the immediate area during construction of the 
PG&E Facility. 
On March 26, 2017, you provided the following additional explanatory information from 
legal documents filed by PG&E. 
• PG&E intends to install a remotely controllable and automated valves system. These 
automated values enhance electronic monitoring of the entire natural gas transmission 
system to identify operational issues, and to prevent pipeline ruptures. Should a rupture 
occur, automated values a1low PG&E to quickly locate, isolate, and minimize damage. 
• Locations for valve automation are selected after an internal review that evaluates a variety 
of factors. For example, PG&E considers factors such as the diameter of the pipe or the gas 
transmission facilities at issue, construction equipment working space requirements, and 
construction working space for the pipe trench and spoil piJe. 


File No. A-17-079 
Page No. 3 
• 
The Property has been identified as the ideal location for a valve station based on the 
alignment of the existing lines and the topography of the site. PG&E has a pair of existing 
easements on the property for transmission lines, however, the easements preclude future 
public or private development of the property. Due to the anticipated scope of construction 
and the installation of the new valve station and facilities, PG&E has determined that it is 
necessary to acquire the entire Property in fee.2 
• 
Additionally, PG&E is dedicated to making its natural gas transmission lines "piggable" 
which means to allow the passage of In-Line Inspection tools. This project will allow PG&E 
to assess the integrity of its natural gas transmission pipelines from the site by running a tool 
inside of the pipeline that can detect damage or corrosion. 
• 
PG&E's present intended scope of work includes the installation of the following facilities: 
Pipes with necessary and proper valves with above-ground gearing, together with such other 
appliances, fittings, devices, equipment, associated supports, concrete pedestals and pads, 
and fixtures necessary to any and all thereof as PG&E deems necessary for the purpose of 
launching and/or receiving internal pipe line devices used to inspect, maintain and monitor 
PG&E's natural gas pipe line system, and such underground wires, cables, conduits, 
appliances, fixtures and appurtenances, as PG&E shall from time to time elect for 
communication purposes, together with adequate protection therefor, and for metering, 
regulating and discharging gas into the atmosphere within, to install concrete curbing on, to 
cover with bitumastic pavement and to enclose with a fence. 
• 
Each of the two gas lines will need to be temporarily taken out of service during 
construction, with each needing to be back online before the weather turns cold. The 
construction schedule has been established and contractors have been retained to perform 
and complete the work before Winter comes. 
• 
Finally, the finished facilities will be landscaped by PG&E to make it fit better into the area. 
PG&E has an arborisl team on staff that will work with the community to determine suitable 
vegetation. PG&E will then hire a contractor to plant this vegetation after the station is 
installed. PG&E will also work with the Home Owners Associations to determine other 
ways of mitigating the visible impacts of this proposed station. 
• 
In the past, PG&E hired appraisers to conduct studies of the effect of similar facilities on the 
property value of neighboring properties. Base on those studies, they do not believe that 
there is evidence that being near such a facility adversely affects property value. 
ANALYSIS 
! PG&E has an existing natural gas valve station located on the Corner of Novato Boulevard and Stone Drive 
within the City of Novato. The ability to expand PG&E's station at that location is not possible due 10 relocation 
impacts of the existing residences adjacent to the station, as well as the traffic control complications involved with 
accessing the new station at an existing high-volume intersection. PG&E's design and safety engineers reviewed a 
number of alternative sites prior to arriving at the property as the most optimal location for the new station. 


File No. A-17-079 
Page No. 4 
The Act's conflict-of-interest rules prohibit a public official from making, participating in 
making, or using his or her official position in any way to influence a governmental decision in 
which the official knows, or has reason to know, that he or she has a "financial interest." (Section 
87 JOO.) A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87103 
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable 
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or 
on any one of five enumerated economic interests in Section 87103(a) through (e). 
Financial bzterest 
The interest here is residential real property in which you have an interest of $2,000 or 
more.3 (Section 87103(b).) 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
A financial effect is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable when it is explicitly involved in 
a decision. Financial interests that are explicitly involved in a decision include an interest that is a 
named party in, or subject of, a government decision, and includes any governmental decision 
affecting a real property financial interest as described in Regulation 18702.2(a)( I H6). (Regulation 
1870J(a).) You are not a named party in, or the subject of, the decisions in question, and the 
decisions are not ones specified in Regulation 18702.2(a)( I )-(6) and therefore you are not explicitly 
involved in the decision. 
For interests that are not explicitly involved, as here, the effect need not be likely to be 
considered reasonably foreseeable. "In general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a 
realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable." 
(Regulation 18701(b).) With respect to materiality and as applicable to real property not explicitly 
involved in a decision, the pertinent standards are if the decision will do the following: 
"(10) Would change the character of the parcel of real property by 
substantially altering traffic levels or intensity of use, including parking, of 
property surrounding the official's real property parcel, the view, privacy, noise 
levels, or air quality, including odors. or any other factors that would affect the 
market value of the real property parcel in which the official has a financial 
interest." 
Your property is 708 feet from the property PG&E is seeking to acquire. Your townhome is 
separated from the subject property (as the crow flies) by Redwood Blvd and Hillside Park Phase 1 
(several buildings with multiple town house units) as well as trees and other landscaping. The City 
Parcel cannot be seen from the townhome. You also noted that: 
• PG&E will landscape the City Parcel outside of the fence for visual screening. 
3 Note that you also have an interest in the tenant as a source of income which is not analyzed herein. Pursuant 
to Regulation l 8702.3(a)(2), the standards in Regulation I 8702.2(b) would apply. 


File No. A-17-079 
Page No. 5 
• It is not anticipated that significant traffic impacts will result from the PG&E Facility 
project in that employees of PG&E will only visit the site occasionally for inspection 
purposes. These inspections will be performed by employees of PG&E who will arrive in 
one or two vehicles and who will stay at the City Parcel a minimum amount of time. Once 
every seven years, PG&E employees will bring in larger equipment and trucks to perform 
certain testing at the City Parcel. 
• It is not likely that significant noise or odor impacts will occur from the operations of the 
PG&E Facility. Once the PG&E Facility is constructed, the public will no longer have 
access to the City Parcel. 
• During construction, it is anticipated that traffic on South Novato Blvd. will be subject to 
traffic control activity which could cause temporary delays during the work day. This 
activity will cease after construction within the roadway and adjacent thereto is complete. 
Noise and dust may be present in the immediate area during construction of the PG&E 
Facility. 
Thus, this materiality standard would not be met. 
In addition, Regulation 18702.2 provides another pertinent materiality standard, the 
foreseeable effect is material if the decision: 
"(12} Would cause a reasonably prudent person, using due care and 
consideration under the circumstances, to believe that the governmental decision 
was of such a nature that its reasonably foreseeable effect would influence the 
market value of the official's property." 
(Regulation 18702.2} 
Most relevant to this analysis would be the fact that PG&E currently holds two easements 
on the subject property and gas lines already run beneath the property. The increased use (from that 
already there) would not be of such a magnitude that it would affect the market value of your 
property. And any safety benefit,; are not localized, but would serve the entire region. 
Therefore, we conclude that you may participate in the decisions because they will not have 
a foreseeable and material financial effect on your real property. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 


JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
File No. A-17-079 
Page No. 6 
nt General Counsel, 
ivision

PG&E to establish a PG&E pipe testing facility despite owning property within 708 feel of the site?

they will not have a foreseeable and material financial effect on your real property.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May you participate in decisions relating to the acquisition of a city-owned parcel by PG&E, 
including decisions relating to the eminent domain proceedings and permits, if any, needed by 
PG&E to establish a PG&E pipe testing facility despite owning property within 708 feel of the site?

CONCLUSION: We conclude, based on the facts presented, that you may participate in the decisions because 
they will not have a foreseeable and material financial effect on your real property.
